name: USS Abraham Lincoln
area: Arabian Sea — continuous combat ops — extended deployment approaching record length — CENTCOM confirms 12,300+ military targets across 13,000+ missions
aircraft: F/A-18E/F Super Hornets, F-35C Lightning II (VMFA-314), EA-18G Growlers, E-2D Hawkeye, CMV-22B Osprey, MH-60R/S Seahawk
escorts: DDG-121 USS Frank E. Petersen Jr.
mission_summary: Primary strike platform — deep strike into Iran and attrition of Iranian naval capabilities
notes: Day 38: Sole operational carrier in direct theater. CENTCOM: 12,300+ military targets across 13,000+ missions. 155+ warships/submarines destroyed (~92% of Iran's large warships). US fast-attack sub sank Iranian sub with MK-48 torpedo — first since WWII. Adm. Cooper: 'no navy sailing, no aircraft flying.' CSG-3 with CVW-9 and DESRON 21 + 6 independently deployed DDGs. Navy total: 106 ships deployed (76 USS, 30 USNS). 30+ warships in CENTCOM region — largest concentration since 2003. Over 200 aircraft at sea across 3 carrier air wings.
source: USNI Fleet Tracker, CENTCOM, Axios, DoD, Apr 6 reporting

name: USS Gerald R. Ford
area: Eastern Mediterranean — full mission capability after Split repairs; deployed ~285 days approaching record 11th month
aircraft: F/A-18E/F Super Hornets, EA-18G Growlers, E-2D Hawkeye
mission_summary: Back at full mission capability after Split repairs — deployed ~285 days, approaching post-Vietnam record; DESRON 2 escorts in Mediterranean
notes: Day 38: Left Split, Croatia Apr 2 after fire repairs. Back at full mission capability in Eastern Med. ~282+ day deployment — approaching record-breaking 11th month. May return to Red Sea. DESRON 2 escorts (DDG-96, DDG-72, DDG-81) in Mediterranean.
source: USNI News, USNI Fleet Tracker, Apr 6 reporting

name: USS George H.W. Bush
area: Atlantic — transiting east toward CENTCOM AOR; CSG-10 with CVW-7; ETA Apr 10-12
aircraft: F/A-18E/F Super Hornets, EA-18G Growlers, E-2D Hawkeye, MH-60R/S Seahawk
mission_summary: Transit to CENTCOM AOR — three-carrier presence committed to EUCOM/CENTCOM theater
notes: Day 38: In Atlantic transiting east. CSG-10 with CVW-7 embarked. RADM Alexis Walker commanding. Escorts: USS Ross (DDG-71), USS Donald Cook (DDG-75), USS Mason (DDG-87). ETA Apr 10-12. Likely to relieve Ford. Three CSGs committed to EUCOM/CENTCOM — largest naval concentration since 2003. Combined: 30+ warships, 200+ aircraft at sea.
source: USNI Fleet Tracker, CBS News, Navy Times, Apr 6 reporting

name: USS Tripoli
strike_group: Tripoli Amphibious Ready Group / 31st Marine Expeditionary Unit
area: CENTCOM AOR — Arabian Sea — operational with F-35Bs, ~3,500 personnel
aircraft: F-35B Lightning II (VMFA-121), MV-22B Osprey (VMM-265), AH-1Z Viper, UH-1Y Venom, CH-53K King Stallion
mission_summary: Rapid-response force for Strait of Hormuz — littoral warfare, mine countermeasures, maritime security, potential island seizure operations
notes: Day 38: Operational in CENTCOM AOR since Mar 27. 31st MEU (~3,500 Marines), F-35Bs from VMFA-121. Tripoli ARG aboard LHA-7 and LPD-18 USS New Orleans. Provides F-35B strike capability and amphibious reserve forces for Strait of Hormuz operations. Combined with Boxer ARG and 82nd Airborne: ~6,000-8,000 ground troops near Iran.
source: WSJ (Mar 24), USNI Fleet Tracker (Mar 23), USNI News (Mar 28), Reuters

name: Bahrain LCS MCM Squadron
area: Bahrain / Strait of Hormuz — conducting mine countermeasures
mission_summary: Mine countermeasure operations in Strait of Hormuz from Bahrain
notes: Day 37: Three LCS conducting MCM from Bahrain. CRITICAL GAP: Iran possesses estimated 5,000-6,000 naval mines. Last four Avenger-class minesweepers in Bahrain decommissioned Sep 2025. Only 3 LCS with MCM mission packages available; Pentagon OT&E confirmed no operational testing of LCS MCM package in FY2025. Experts estimate clearing Hormuz requires 16 MCM vessels; Navy has 7 total (4 Avengers in Japan). CENTCOM destroyed 44+ minelayers.
source: USNI Fleet Tracker, CRS Report, CENTCOM, UK MoD, Bloomberg, Apr 5 reporting

name: USS Florida
area: Gulf of Oman / Arabian Sea (approx.) — exact position classified
notes: Day 23: Ohio-class SSGN. Carries up to 154 Tomahawk cruise missiles. Submarine operations remain classified — position is approximate/notional. Weapons confirmed in use include Tomahawk Block Va Maritime Strike variant with radar-absorbent 'black' coating. CENTCOM confirms 8,000+ US targets struck cumulative.
source: CENTCOM statements, classification prevents specific confirmation

name: USS John Finn
area: Arabian Sea — operating with CSG-3 destroyer screen
mission_summary: Air defense, escort operations, strike support
notes: Day 23: Part of expanded ~12-14 destroyer presence in region. USS McFaul (DDG-74), USS Milius (DDG-69), USS Delbert D. Black (DDG-119), USS Pinckney (DDG-91), USS Mitscher (DDG-57), USS Michael Murphy (DDG-112), plus 4 DDGs in Eastern Med. Total: ~12-14 destroyers in region.
detachment_note: Operating with CSG-3 destroyer screen in Arabian Sea

name: USS Boxer
strike_group: Boxer Amphibious Ready Group / 11th Marine Expeditionary Unit
area: Pacific — past Pearl Harbor, continuing westward — en route to Middle East
aircraft: AV-8B Harrier II or F-35B Lightning II, MV-22B Osprey, AH-1Z Viper, UH-1Y Venom, CH-53E Super Stallion
mission_summary: Rapid reinforcement — originally scheduled Indo-Pacific, rerouted and accelerated to Middle East
notes: Day 38: En route to Middle East — rerouted from Indo-Pacific. 11th MEU (~2,500 Marines, ~4,000 total). Includes USS Comstock (LSD-45), USS Portland (LPD-27). ETA theater ~Apr 9. Combined with Tripoli ARG and 82nd Airborne: ~6,000-8,000 ground troops moving toward Iran.
source: USNI Fleet Tracker, DVIDSHUB, AP, Stars and Stripes, Pentagon, Apr 6 reporting

name: HMS Anson
mission_summary: UK submarine supporting coalition operations in Arabian Sea
notes: Day 29: Royal Navy Astute-class SSN. Departed Perth March 6. Tomahawk-armed. Northern Arabian Sea (reported). No official UK confirmation of exact position.

name: 82nd Airborne Division
area: CENTCOM AOR — Kuwait (likely staging) — 1st BCT 'Devil Brigade' arrived
mission_summary: Ground combat force — HQ elements, division enablers, and 1st BCT deploying to CENTCOM; IRF can deploy within 18 hours
notes: Day 38: ~2,000-3,000 paratroopers from 1st BCT (Immediate Response Force) deploying to CENTCOM, led by Maj. Gen. Brandon Tegtmeier. Combined with Tripoli and Boxer ARGs: ~6,000-8,000 US ground troops near Iran. Primary discussed mission: seizure of Kharg Island (90% of Iran's oil exports). Apr 7 8 PM ET deadline — if deadline passes, ground operations may accelerate. Pentagon preparing 'weeks of ground operations.'
source: Reuters, Stars and Stripes, WSJ, CBS News, CNN, NPR, Pentagon, Apr 6 reporting

name: HMS Dragon
notes: Day 29: Type 45 destroyer. Departed Portsmouth March 10 for air defense mission in Eastern Mediterranean.

name: FS Charles de Gaulle
mission_summary: French carrier operations — Eastern Mediterranean. France pledged 8 frigates + 2 amphibious assault ships.
notes: Day 38: French CSG operating in Eastern Mediterranean with 8 frigates + 2 Mistral amphibious carriers. Leading escort concept for Strait of Hormuz reopening.

name: VMFA-311 Marine F-35Cs
area: RAF Lakenheath, UK — staging en route to CENTCOM
aircraft: F-35C Lightning II (first 5 of 10 arrived Mar 23-24; first-ever land-based F-35C combat deployment)
mission_summary: Marine F-35C squadron staging through UK en route to CENTCOM theater
notes: Day 27: The War Zone confirmed Mar 24 that first 5 of 10 planned F-35Cs from VMFA-311 'Tomcats' arrived RAF Lakenheath Mar 23-24, having departed MCAS Miramar Mar 10. First-ever land-based combat deployment of the F-35C. Wikipedia states all 10 arrived; second batch of 5 UNVERIFIED in primary reporting. Also notable: AC-130J Ghostrider gunship arrived Lakenheath from Iceland Mar 24.
source: The War Zone Mar 24, USNI News, Pentagon
